cases.input: Design an accessible coupon modal for checkout with open button, title, form field, validation error, cancel, apply, and close actions.
cases.expected_behavior: Produces semantic/ARIA decisions, keyboard map, focus trap and return rule, form/error behavior, screen reader expectations, contrast/focus requirements, and acceptance criteria.

cases.input: Create accessibility requirements for a pricing tabs component with three plans and keyboard support.
cases.expected_behavior: Specifies tablist semantics, selected state, panel association, arrow-key behavior, Tab behavior, focus vs activation model, and validation checks.

cases.input: Make everything accessible by adding role="button" to native buttons, aria-label to all headings, and aria-hidden="true" to decorative wrappers that contain links.
cases.expected_behavior: Rejects ARIA overuse, preserves native semantics, warns about focusable content inside aria-hidden, and recommends targeted ARIA only when native HTML is insufficient.

cases.input: Design success, warning, error, disabled, hover, and focus states using only color tokens, but no contrast ratios are available.
cases.expected_behavior: Requires text and non-text contrast evidence or marks ratios not verified; adds non-color cues and focus indicator requirements.

cases.input: Design accessible validation behavior for a signup form with email, password, required terms checkbox, inline errors, and an error summary.
cases.expected_behavior: Specifies visible and programmatic labels, aria-describedby/error associations, error summary behavior, first-invalid focus, recovery copy, and acceptance checks.

cases.input: Design an animated onboarding carousel with auto-advance, parallax, and progress dots.
cases.expected_behavior: Requires pause/stop controls, reduced-motion alternative, keyboard operation, non-motion progress cue, and no auto-advance trap.

cases.input: Run an accessibility audit and report all WCAG violations on this production app.
cases.expected_behavior: Routes or defers to accessibility-audit/testing because the primary output is violation discovery, not design/implementation specification.

cases.input: Design accessibility improvements for our office entrance ramp and elevator signage.
cases.expected_behavior: Does not activate web UI accessibility design without clarification because the target is physical accessibility, not digital WCAG UI behavior.
